hotspots.title: Lincoln Homestead sign
hotspots.description: A small sign in the upper-left corner labels the Lincoln Homestead, setting off one of the rural history vignettes around the edge of the Harrisonburg scene.

hotspots.title: Horse-drawn wagon
hotspots.description: A horse-drawn wagon moves along the upper-left roadway, part of the map's old-time rural imagery and transportation jokes.

hotspots.title: Red barn on hillside
hotspots.description: A red barn sits on the hillside near the top of the illustration, marking the agricultural landscape around Harrisonburg and Rockingham County.

hotspots.title: Boat with “I don’t believe it!” gag
hotspots.description: A small fishing boat gag appears on the water with the caption, "I don't believe it!" The joke is one of the tiny comic scenes tucked into the map.

hotspots.title: U.S. Route 42 marker
hotspots.description: The U.S. Route 42 marker identifies one of the major roads threading through the illustrated town-and-county landscape.

hotspots.title: Poultry Festival animals
hotspots.description: A group of animals stands near the Poultry Festival reference, nodding to the region's poultry industry and festival culture.

hotspots.title: “This is udderly impossible” cow
hotspots.description: A cow-centered visual pun reads as an "udderly impossible" gag, one of the map's playful animal jokes.

hotspots.title: Court Square Village storefront sign
hotspots.description: A storefront sign identifies Court Square Village, placing the viewer near the downtown commercial core.

hotspots.title: Eastern Mennonite College buildings
hotspots.description: This cluster marks Eastern Mennonite College, now Eastern Mennonite University, with campus buildings drawn into the upper-right portion of the map.

hotspots.title: Large bird above Eastern Mennonite College
hotspots.description: A large bird flies above the Eastern Mennonite College area, functioning as a small visual landmark in the upper-right sky.

hotspots.title: Village blacksmith / anvil gag
hotspots.description: A village blacksmith scene with an anvil appears among the downtown-area vignettes, adding an old-town trade reference and a bit of slapstick.

hotspots.title: Joseph Neys building
hotspots.description: A building labeled for Joseph Neys appears in the older village/downtown portion of the illustration.

hotspots.title: Basketball player statue
hotspots.description: A basketball-player figure or statue stands near the Harrisonburg High School area, calling attention to school spirit and athletics.

hotspots.title: c.1750 Bruce Street house
hotspots.description: The c. 1750 Bruce Street house appears here as one of the historical-house references embedded in the drawing.

hotspots.title: “Saved by JMU” character
hotspots.description: A character labeled "Saved by JMU" appears as a small joke in the central part of the map, near James Madison University references.

hotspots.title: Radio tower
hotspots.description: A radio tower rises above nearby buildings, a thin landmark that is easy to miss unless you scan the skyline carefully.

hotspots.title: Harrisonburg Baptist Church
hotspots.description: Harrisonburg Baptist Church is shown as a prominent church building in the downtown/civic portion of the illustration.

hotspots.title: Asbury United Methodist Church
hotspots.description: Asbury United Methodist Church appears as another labeled church landmark in the central Harrisonburg scene.

hotspots.title: Municipal Building
hotspots.description: The Municipal Building appears as a civic landmark near the churches and downtown institutions in the center of the map.

hotspots.title: Valley Memorial Hospital sign
hotspots.description: A sign for Valley Memorial Hospital identifies the hospital area before the newer Rockingham Memorial Hospital reference elsewhere on the map.

hotspots.title: Massanutten Bank & Trust small branch
hotspots.description: A small Massanutten Bank & Trust branch is shown among the central business and civic landmarks.

hotspots.title: Valley Civic Center storefront
hotspots.description: The Valley Civic Center storefront appears in the downtown commercial strip, one of several labeled businesses and public venues.

hotspots.title: James Madison University main building
hotspots.description: The main James Madison University building is drawn as a major campus landmark, anchoring the JMU portion of the map.

hotspots.title: JMU front-lawn figures
hotspots.description: Small figures on the James Madison University front lawn add activity and humor to the campus scene.

hotspots.title: Löwenbräu truck
hotspots.description: A Löwenbräu truck drives near the JMU and hospital area, one of several delivery vehicles hidden in the illustration.

hotspots.title: Rockingham Memorial Hospital
hotspots.description: Rockingham Memorial Hospital is labeled with its large hospital complex, one of the key institutional landmarks in the lower half of the map.

hotspots.title: Red earth / construction area near hospital
hotspots.description: A red-earth construction area near Rockingham Memorial Hospital suggests campus or hospital expansion work in progress.

hotspots.title: Valley Mall
hotspots.description: Valley Mall is shown as a large retail landmark, capturing the commercial growth and shopping culture of Harrisonburg in the early 1980s.

hotspots.title: Zaggers sign
hotspots.description: A small Zaggers sign appears near the Valley Mall area, one of the many local business labels scattered through the scene.

hotspots.title: Large rooster at lower left
hotspots.description: A large rooster dominates the lower-left area, a playful nod to poultry and rural Rockingham County identity.

hotspots.title: Swing set in Purcell Park
hotspots.description: A small swing set in Purcell Park marks a recreational detail in the green park area.

hotspots.title: Small airplane in Purcell Park
hotspots.description: A small airplane appears in the Purcell Park area, one of the whimsical objects hidden in the lower-left landscape.

hotspots.title: Horse and bull gag
hotspots.description: A horse-and-bull scene forms a compact comic gag near the lower-left side of the illustration.

hotspots.title: Downtown row including Wilson Jewelers
hotspots.description: A downtown storefront row includes Wilson Jewelers and neighboring businesses, representing the dense commercial fabric around Court Square.

hotspots.title: Jess’ Quick Lunch
hotspots.description: Jess' Quick Lunch is marked in the downtown row, highlighting a familiar local eatery in the city-center business district.

hotspots.title: Rockingham National Bank storefront
hotspots.description: A Rockingham National Bank storefront appears among the downtown buildings, one of several banking references in the map.

hotspots.title: Large courthouse-area building
hotspots.description: A large courthouse-area building anchors the downtown block near Court Square, the flag, and the surrounding storefronts.

hotspots.title: American flag at Court Square
hotspots.description: The American flag at Court Square marks the civic center of downtown Harrisonburg and helps orient the viewer in the courthouse area.

hotspots.title: Confederate monument
hotspots.description: The Confederate monument is shown at Court Square. The title should be reviewed for present-day interpretive language and historical accuracy.

hotspots.title: The Outfitters yellow arch
hotspots.description: A yellow arch marks The Outfitters area, a bright visual cue in the Rolling Hills Shopping Center section.

hotspots.title: Small yellow buggy
hotspots.description: A small yellow buggy sits along the roadway, another transportation detail in the busy shopping-center area.

hotspots.title: East Market Street flower-shaped road
hotspots.description: The East Market Street area is drawn with a flower-like road or interchange shape, a distinctive piece of the map's playful cartography.

hotspots.title: Wooden bank building
hotspots.description: A wooden bank building is drawn near the cluster of financial institutions, likely a historical or stylized business reference.

hotspots.title: Rockingham Savings & Loan bottom building
hotspots.description: A red-roofed Rockingham Savings & Loan building appears in the lower banking district, near the curved roads and commercial buildings.

hotspots.title: Parus & Associates copyright area
hotspots.description: The Parus & Associates copyright area identifies the mapmaker/publisher credit printed near the lower part of the illustration.

hotspots.title: Rockingham National Bank curved building
hotspots.description: A curved Rockingham National Bank building anchors part of the lower-right commercial corridor.

hotspots.title: Valley National Bank
hotspots.description: Valley National Bank is shown as a labeled bank building in the lower-right business district.

hotspots.title: Walmart storefront
hotspots.description: A Walmart storefront appears among the lower-right retail buildings, capturing a recognizable national retailer in the local commercial landscape.

hotspots.title: Rockingham National Bank red building
hotspots.description: A red Rockingham National Bank building appears near the lower-right banking and retail cluster.

hotspots.title: Rockingham Savings & Loan upper building
hotspots.description: Rockingham Savings & Loan appears as an upper building in the lower-right commercial area.

hotspots.title: Dairy truck
hotspots.description: A dairy truck is drawn near the lower-right roadway, a local delivery detail tied to the region's food and agriculture economy.

hotspots.title: Hess & Miller Inc. sign
hotspots.description: The Hess & Miller Inc. sign labels a business in the lower-right industrial or commercial cluster.

hotspots.title: Hess & Miller building
hotspots.description: The Hess & Miller building sits beside its sign, adding another local business landmark to the lower-right edge.

hotspots.title: Lite beer truck
hotspots.description: A Lite beer truck appears along the lower-right roadway, one of the map's many tiny vehicle and delivery details.

hotspots.title: Welcome to Harrisonburg sign and eagle
hotspots.description: The Welcome to Harrisonburg sign frames the lower-right entrance to the illustrated city scene, with an eagle flying beneath it.

hotspots.title: Racehorse and rider scene
hotspots.description: A racehorse and rider scene appears near the bottom edge, another small action vignette in the map's outer band.

hotspots.title: City of Harrisonburg seal
hotspots.description: The City Seal of Harrisonburg is printed as an official civic emblem in the lower-right portion of the map.

hotspots.title: Tree-trunk speech gag
hotspots.description: A tree-trunk speech gag appears near the lower-right corner, one of the more hidden cartoon captions in the map.

hotspots.title: Hunter at lower right
hotspots.description: A hunter figure appears near the bottom-right edge, part of a small outdoor or sporting vignette.

hotspots.title: Cannon at far right
hotspots.description: A cannon is drawn near the far-right edge, adding a historical or military-reference detail to the outer scene.

hotspots.title: “10-4 good buddy” turkey hunter note
hotspots.description: A small note reads like a CB-radio joke: "10-4 good buddy... got my sights on Turkeytown!" The nearby hunter makes the punchline easier to spot.

hotspots.title: Joseph Neys street scene
hotspots.description: This wider downtown street scene includes the Joseph Neys building, nearby pedestrians, and a roadside gag about a large race purse.

hotspots.title: U.S. 33 office and hunter gag
hotspots.description: This cluster combines a U.S. Route 33 marker, an office-like building, and the nearby turkey-hunter joke at the edge of town.

hotspots.title: Cannon and hillside barn
hotspots.description: A cannon sits near a hillside and red barn detail on the far-right edge of the illustration, mixing historic imagery with rural scenery.
